## 💡 Goal
We want to enrich our English idioms collection by adding more examples that help language learners understand everyday expressions.

## 🎯 Your Task
Add the following English ID entry at the end of our IDs list.

### 🪶 The ID
| Field | Content |
|-------|----------|
| **ID** | polish the apple |
| **Phonetic** | /ˈpɒlɪʃ ðə ˈæpəl/ |
| **Persian Meaning** | پاچه خواری کردن (خودشیرینی کردن) |
| **Example (HTML‑ready)** | Nobody likes her because she is always trying to polish the apple with her teacher. |
| **Example Meaning (Farsi)** | هیچکس او را دوست ندارد چون او همیشه سعی می کند برای معلمش خودشیرینی کند. |

1. ⭐ **Star** our repository

   ```json
     "idiom": "polish the apple",

   }
   ```

5. Make sure to **add a comma** after the previous object to keep the JSON valid.
6. 💾 Click *Commit changes* to save.
7. 🚀 Create a **Pull Request** with the title:

8. Link this issue using `Closes #<issue_number>`.
9. Wait for review — and you're an open‑source contributor! 🎉

3. Open [`community/content/japanese-cultural-etiquette.json`](../blob/main/community/content/japanese-cultural-etiquette.json) in your browser (click the link!)
4. Scroll to the bottom of the file and paste the following cultural etiquette object just before the closing `]`:

```json
{
  "situation": "公共スペース 4",
  "do": "ゴミを持ち帰る",
  "dont": "ゴミを放置する",
  "note": "ゴミ箱が少ない地域がある。"
}
```

5. Add a comma after the previous last entry in the array (so the JSON stays valid)
6. Save the file and commit the changes
7. Submit a Pull Request with title: `content: add new cultural etiquette tip`
8. Link this issue using `Closes #<issue_number>`
9. Wait for review!

Goal

Add a short newcomer-sized note to the JavaScript spreadsheet library guide that explains when Handsontable-style browser grids are the right first tool, and where a headless WorkPaper runtime starts being useful instead.

## Suggested files

- `docs/javascript-spreadsheet-library-headless-node.md`
- optional: `docs/headless-spreadsheet-engine-comparison.md` if you need one related link

## Suggested approach

Keep this docs-only. Add one paragraph or one table-row clarification under the grid/browser-editing path. The important distinction is: a browser grid owns UI behavior such as rendering, copy/paste, selection, validation, and editing; `@bilig/headless` owns server-side workbook state, formulas, persistence, and post-write readback.

Use neutral language. Do not claim Handsontable is worse, and do not imply bilig is a visual grid.

## Acceptance proof

- The guide has a clear Handsontable/grid boundary note.
- The note links back to `@bilig/headless` only when the job is server-side workbook mutation or readback.
- Run `pnpm docs:discovery:check`.

### 🎯 Your Task

Add this grammar explanation to our learner-friendly grammar list!

### The Grammar Point

> "〜てもらう" means "receive a favor" from someone.

### 📝 Instructions

1. Star our repo ⭐
2. Fork our repo 🍴
3. Open [`community/content/japanese-grammar.json`](../blob/main/community/content/japanese-grammar.json) in your browser (click the link!)
4. Scroll to the bottom of the file and paste the following grammar string just before the closing `]`:

```json
""〜てもらう" means "receive a favor" from someone."
```

5. Add a comma after the previous last entry in the array (so the JSON stays valid)
6. Save the file and commit the changes
7. Submit a Pull Request with title: `content: add new grammar point`
8. Link this issue using `Closes #<issue_number>`
9. Wait for review!

Add this traditional Japanese proverb to help learners understand Japanese wisdom!

### The Proverb

| Japanese | Reading | English |
|----------|---------|---------|
| **縁は異なもの味なもの** | En wa inamono aji na mono | Fate is strange yet delightful |

> 💡 **Meaning:** Unexpected connections can be good

```json
{
  "japanese": "縁は異なもの味なもの",
  "romaji": "En wa inamono aji na mono",
  "english": "Fate is strange yet delightful",
  "meaning": "Unexpected connections can be good"
}
```
